new_classes.label_en: Other Wine of Fresh Grapes (no PDO/PGI) in containers > 2 L (CN 2204 22 98)
new_classes.label_de: Anderer Wein aus frischen Weintrauben (ohne g.U./g.g.A.) in Behältnissen > 2 l (KN 2204 22 98)
new_classes.definition_en: Still wine of fresh grapes bearing no Protected Designation of Origin (PDO) and no Protected Geographical Indication (PGI), presented in containers of more than 2 litres and up to 10 litres, classified under CN subheading 2204 22 98. The total dry extract may contain sugars, glycerol, tannins, tartaric acid, colouring matter and salts as non-volatile constituents within the meaning of additional note 4(A) to Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The axioms capture the three main distinguishing criteria for CN 2204 22 98: (1) wine of fresh grapes (via parent class WineOfFreshGrapes), (2) absence of PDO/PGI (via complement restrictions on PDOOriginQuality and PGIOriginQuality), and (3) large container > 2 L (via someValuesFrom LargeContainer, which is defined in the TBox as ≥ 2 L — the exact upper bound of ≤ 10 L for this subheading versus > 10 L for CN 2204 29 cannot be expressed without a dedicated container size range class or data range restriction on the volume, which is not available in the current TBox without introducing a new named class for 'containers > 2 L and ≤ 10 L'). The reference to total dry extract non-volatile constituents (additional note 4(A)) is captured by the TotalDryExtractQuality restriction as an acknowledgement of the classification criterion, but the precise g/L thresholds referenced in additional notes 4 and 5 are not fully modelled here because those threshold values for the 2204 22 series are not determinable from the note text provided alone. Coverage is therefore partial: container size lower bound (> 2 L) is correctly expressed via LargeContainer; the upper bound (≤ 10 L) and additional-note dry-extract thresholds are not quantitatively constrained.
